DELETE /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce.description: Delete all rules in an account.

parameters.description: The unique identifier for the rule.
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The unique identifier for the rule.

commit_message.description: Human-readable justification for the deletion. Required for internal-account submissions; optional for customer accounts and automated sync.

400.description: Validation error (e.g. commit_message exceeds max length).

DELETE /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce.description: Remove regex patterns from per-account exemption rules. Missing keys leave that type untouched; non-existent patterns are silently skipped.

200.description: Full exemption state after the subtraction.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce-on.description: List all rules for an account with optional filtering.

parameters.description: Filter by namespace. Repeat the parameter to filter by multiple namespaces (e.g. namespace=foo&namespace=bar).
parameters.name: namespace

schema.description: Filter by namespace. Repeat the parameter to filter by multiple namespaces (e.g. namespace=foo&namespace=bar).

parameters.description: Filter by path using exact-match semantics (no descendant matching). Omit the parameter to return rules from all paths. Pass an empty string (path=) to return only rules with an empty/uncategorized path. Pass a non-empty value (e.g. path=yara) for an exact match against that path. Repeat the parameter (e.g. path=yara&path=expr) to OR-match across multiple paths (SQL `IN (...)` semantics). Note: the `recursive` flag does NOT affect path filtering — it only affects namespace filtering for customer accounts.
parameters.name: path

schema.description: Filter by path using exact-match semantics (no descendant matching). Omit the parameter to return rules from all paths. Pass an empty string (path=) to return only rules with an empty/uncategorized path. Pass a non-empty value (e.g. path=yara) for an exact match against that path. Repeat the parameter (e.g. path=yara&path=expr) to OR-match across multiple paths (SQL `IN (...)` semantics). Note: the `recursive` flag does NOT affect path filtering — it only affects namespace filtering for customer accounts.

parameters.description: Customer accounts only: when true, namespace filtering matches descendants. Does NOT affect path filtering (paths are always exact-match).
parameters.name: recursive

schema.description: Customer accounts only: when true, namespace filtering matches descendants. Does NOT affect path filtering (paths are always exact-match).

enabled.description: Whether this rule is active for dice consumers.

is_public.description: Whether this rule is visible to other internal accounts.

meta.description: Structured meta entries for the rule (parsed from content plus any request-supplied meta). Returned in source order.

pending_approval_id.description: ID of an open approval workflow targeting this rule, or null if none is pending.

structured_source.description: Original JSON payload for rules created via the structured rules API. Null for hand-written rules.

parameters.description: Retrieval strategy used for the query.

schema.description: Retrieval strategy used for the query.

fallback.description: True when AI Search was unavailable and the response is from a fallback path.

interpreted.description: Parsed natural-language interpretation of the query, when available.

filters.description: Filters applied during retrieval (account ACL plus user-supplied facets).

mode.description: Retrieval strategy actually used to produce results.

enabled.description: Whether this rule is active for dice consumers.

is_public.description: Whether this rule is visible to other internal accounts.

pending_approval_id.description: ID of an open approval workflow targeting this rule, or null if none is pending.

score.description: Relevance score in [0,1]. Present only when AI Search powers the query.

scoring_details.description: Per-component scoring breakdown returned by AI Search hybrid retrieval.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce-on.description: Get statistics about rules for the dashboard.
Get the folder tree structure for rules navigation.

commit_message.description: Human-readable justification for this change. Required for internal-account submissions; optional for customer accounts and automated sync.
commit_message.example: Add worker rule to catch bpb-panel variants.

description.description: Human-readable description of the rule. Auto-extracted from YARA meta if present.

enabled.description: Whether this rule is active for dice consumers.

is_public.description: Whether this rule is visible to other internal accounts.

meta.description: Additional YARA meta entries appended to the rule's meta block (and stored in rule_meta alongside meta parsed from the content). Keys must be valid YARA identifiers and must not be 'name', 'enabled', or 'description'. Duplicate keys are allowed.

items.description: A YARA meta entry. Value type is resolved server-side. Constrained keys: 'detection' must be one of MALICIOUS, SUSPICIOUS, SPAM, SPOOF.

namespaces.description: Optional WfP deployment tags (customer rules only). Internal rules leave empty.

enabled.description: Whether this rule is active for dice consumers.

is_public.description: Whether this rule is visible to other internal accounts.

meta.description: Structured meta entries for the rule (parsed from content plus any request-supplied meta). Returned in source order.

pending_approval_id.description: ID of an open approval workflow targeting this rule, or null if none is pending.

structured_source.description: Original JSON payload for rules created via the structured rules API. Null for hand-written rules.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce-o.description: Add regex patterns to per-account exemption rules (union semantics). Missing keys leave that type untouched; duplicates are silently deduped.

200.description: Full exemption state after the union.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce-o.description: Validate rule syntax, name uniqueness, namespace, and meta checks.

meta.description: Additional YARA meta entries appended to the rule's meta block (and stored in rule_meta alongside meta parsed from the content). Keys must be valid YARA identifiers and must not be 'name', 'enabled', or 'description'. Duplicate keys are allowed.

path.description: Path change goes through approval workflow.

PUT /accounts/{account_id}/cloudforce-on.description: Replace existing exemption patterns with new values. Each key maps to an array of {old_pattern, new_pattern} entries. Missing keys leave that type untouched. Fails if any old pattern is not found or any new pattern already exists.

200.description: Full exemption state after the updates.
